title: JNDI Injection in an Outdated Logging Library
objective: Trigger remote code execution via a known JNDI-injection flaw in the victim's outdated logging library.
briefing: The victim server runs a logging library, version 2.14.1, that logs HTTP headers unsanitized - including the User-Agent header.
hints: Check what's doing the logging, and what version it is - is it current?
That version has a well-known remote-code-execution flaw triggered by a lookup syntax in logged text.
debrief: The logging library evaluates ${jndi:...} lookup syntax found inside any string it logs - including HTTP headers you fully control. By sending that syntax in the User-Agent header, the server made an outbound lookup to an attacker-controlled address, which in a real attack would return a malicious class to execute (this is the Log4Shell vulnerability, CVE-2021-44228). This is a Vulnerable and Outdated Component (OWASP A06:2021): the flaw wasn't in code the developers wrote, it was in a dependency they never updated. The fix is upgrading past the vulnerable version (and disabling JNDI lookups by default), plus a process for tracking and patching dependency CVEs instead of running whatever version was installed once.

cases.output:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
(server made outbound {proto} lookup while logging the request)

logs_append: [VULN] log4j 2.14.1 evaluated JNDI lookup from User-Agent header - RCE triggered
